metadata.title: Online Shopping
metadata.subtitle: Shop Smart, Stay Safe, and Protect Your Money in the Digital Marketplace

content.title: Online Shopping
content.subtitle: Shop Smart, Stay Safe, and Protect Your Money in the Digital Marketplace

content.label: The Challenge
content.question:
Mike found a popular gaming console on an unfamiliar website for 40% below retail price. Excited by the deal, he purchased it without researching the site.

After payment, he received only an automated email. The console never arrived.

When he tried to contact customer service, the website had disappeared. He lost $300 before his credit card company helped him dispute the charge.

What warning signs did Mike miss, and how could he have protected himself from this scam?

objectives.description: Identify the benefits and risks of online shopping compared to in-store purchases.
Evaluate website security features to determine if a retailer is trustworthy.
Apply strategies to protect personal and financial information when shopping online.
Recognize red flags and common online shopping scams before falling victim.

terms.definition: Secure website protocol that encrypts data between your browser and the website. Look for 'https://' and a padlock icon.
terms.example: Amazon.com uses https://www.amazon.com—notice the padlock and 'https' indicating encryption.

terms.definition: Fraudulent attempts to steal personal information by impersonating legitimate businesses through fake emails, texts, or websites.
terms.example: An email claiming to be from your bank asking you to 'verify' your account by clicking a suspicious link.

terms.definition: An electronic service (Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal) that adds a security layer between your payment info and merchants.
terms.example: Using PayPal means the seller never sees your actual credit card number—PayPal acts as a middleman.

terms.definition: Extra security requiring two forms of verification—something you know (password) plus something you have (phone code).
terms.example: After entering your password, you also enter a code texted to your phone—even if someone has your password, they can't access your account.

leftColumn.title: BENEFITS
items: No travel time or parking hassles
Often lower prices than physical stores
Access to products not available locally

rightColumn.title: RISKS
items: Returns may be difficult or costly

content.headerTitle: Red Flags: Signs of a Scam Website

layoutData.title: Warning Signs Mike Missed
paragraphs: Learn to spot these danger signals before you buy:
bulletPoints: No HTTPS/padlock: URL shows 'http://' without padlock—data isn't encrypted
Prices too good to be true: 40-60% below retail? Legitimate retailers can't sustain those discounts
No contact information: Missing physical address, phone number, or working email
Limited payment options: Only accepts wire transfers, gift cards, or cryptocurrency—no credit cards
Poor website quality: Broken links, spelling errors, blurry images, copied content
Recently created domain: Website registered days or weeks ago (check whois lookup)
No return policy: Or vague policy with no actual process

keyPoint.text: 🚨 Mike's site had MULTIPLE red flags: unfamiliar retailer, 40% discount, no verification. One red flag = caution. Multiple = avoid!

layoutData.title: What to Look For Before Buying
bulletPoints: HTTPS and padlock: Encrypted connection protects your data
Verifiable contact info: Physical address, phone number, customer service email
Multiple payment options: Major credit cards, PayPal, Apple Pay—not just wire transfers
Clear return policy: Specific timeframes, process, and conditions
Customer reviews: On the site AND on external review sites (Trustpilot, BBB)
Professional appearance: No obvious errors, working links, clear product descriptions
Established presence: Company has been around, can be researched

keyPoint.text: ✓ When in doubt, Google '[company name] reviews' or '[company name] scam' before purchasing.

content.headerTitle: Credit Cards vs. Debit Cards Online

leftColumn.title: CREDIT CARDS (Safer)
items: Not connected to your bank account

layoutData.title: Your Security Checklist
paragraphs: Follow these practices to protect yourself:
bulletPoints: Verify the website: HTTPS, contact info, reviews, realistic prices
Use credit cards: Better fraud protection than debit cards
Consider digital wallets: PayPal, Apple Pay add a security layer
Use unique passwords: Different password for each shopping site
Enable two-factor authentication: Extra verification for your accounts
Check statements regularly: Catch unauthorized charges quickly
Save confirmations: Keep email receipts and order confirmations
Use a separate email: Dedicated email for shopping reduces spam and phishing risk

keyPoint.text: 🔒 If you wouldn't hand a stranger your credit card in person, don't do it online with an unverified site.

leftPanel.title: Scams to Watch For
paragraphs: Social media ads: 'Going out of business' sales that never deliver
Too-good deals: Popular items at 50-70% off retail
Fake reviews: All 5-star reviews with similar language
Bait and switch: Advertise one product, send inferior substitute

infoCard.title: If You're Scammed:
items: Contact your credit card company immediately

content.headerTitle: Online vs. In-Store: When to Choose Each

leftPanel.title: Shop ONLINE When:
paragraphs: • You know exactly what you want
• You want to compare prices easily

infoCard.title: Shop IN-STORE When:
items: You need to see/try before buying
Big purchase you want to inspect
Local business you want to support

scenario.name: Website Evaluation: BargainTech-Deals.net
paragraphs: URL shows 'http://' (no padlock). Prices are 60% below retail. No physical address or phone number listed.
Only accepts wire transfers or gift cards. Website was registered 2 weeks ago. No customer reviews found.

scenario.name: Website Evaluation: LocalBoutique.com
paragraphs: URL shows 'https://' with padlock. Small business you've never heard of. Has physical address you can verify on Google Maps.
Accepts major credit cards and PayPal. Limited reviews (12 on site, 8 on Google). Clear return policy—30 days.

content.question:
What THREE things should Mike have done differently before purchasing from that unfamiliar website?

How would each action have protected him from losing $300?

Describe your personal approach to online shopping:

• What steps do you take (or will you start taking) to protect yourself?
• What would make you decide NOT to complete a purchase?
• Have you or someone you know ever been scammed online?

questions.question: Which of these is a sign that a website may NOT be secure: HTTPS, padlock icon, only accepts gift cards, or customer reviews?
Why is it generally safer to pay with a credit card rather than a debit card when shopping online?
List three red flags that Mike missed on the scam website.
Why is using a separate email for online shopping a smart security practice?

takeaways.title: Verify Before You Buy
takeaways.description: Check for HTTPS, contact info, reviews, and realistic prices. Multiple red flags = avoid the site.

takeaways.title: Use Credit Cards Online
takeaways.description: Credit cards offer better fraud protection than debit cards. Your money isn't immediately at risk.

takeaways.title: Too Good = Too Risky
takeaways.description: Prices 40-60% below retail are usually scams. Legitimate retailers can't sustain those discounts.

takeaways.title: When in Doubt, Research
takeaways.description: Google '[company] reviews' or '[company] scam' before buying from unfamiliar sites.

main.title: Website Security Audit Challenge
main.description: Mike learned the hard way—but can you spot the scams before getting burned? Tomorrow you'll evaluate real and fake websites, identify red flags, and create your personal online shopping security checklist.

steps: Evaluate website security features and identify trustworthy vs. risky sites
Spot red flags in shopping scenarios (pricing, payment methods, contact info)
Compare online vs. in-store advantages for different purchase types
Analyze what Mike should have done differently
Create your personal online shopping security checklist

content.tagline: Building Financial Futures, One Lesson at a Time
